questions.question: Which among the following is the correct set of chemical formulae of Chile Saltpetre, Saltpetre and Quick Lime?
questions.explanation: Saltpetre, also spelled Saltpeter, also called Nitre, or Niter, any of three naturally occurring nitrates, distinguished as (1) ordinary saltpetre, or potassium nitrate, KNO3; (2) Chile saltpetre, cubic nitre, or sodium nitrate, NaNO3; and (3) lime saltpetre, wall saltpetre, or calcium nitrate, Ca(NO3)2. Calcium oxide (CaO), commonly known as quicklime or burnt lime, is a widely used chemical compound.

questions.question: Which among the following compound is most commonly used in the Photochromic lenses?
questions.explanation: Photochromatic or Photochromic glass acquires a darker shade when exposed to bright light and returns to its original lighter shade in dim light. This happens because silver halides (iodide or chloride) is added to this glass

questions.question: Highest percentage of nitrogen is found in which among the following fertilizers among the given options?
questions.explanation: The agricultural industry widely uses urea, a white crystalline solid containing 46 percent nitrogen as an animal feed additive and fertilizer. Urea has the highest nitrogen content of all solid fertilizers at 46% N.

questions.question: In what form is Nitrogen present in Urea?
questions.explanation: Urea is an organic compound with the formula (NH2)2CO. The Nitrogen present in Urea is in the form of Amide. This compound is highly soluble in water and is a very effective source of Nitrogen for plants. It is the main nitrogenous end product of the metabolic breakdown of proteins in all mammals and some fishes.

questions.question: Which among the following is known as Quick Lime?
questions.explanation: Calcium oxide (CaO), commonly known as quicklime or burnt lime, is a widely used chemical compound. It is a white, caustic, alkaline crystalline solid at room temperature.
